rulings.Case Title: JAY WANG VS. ORACLE AMERICA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award (Notices)
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion/Discovery calendar for Monday, May 13, 2026, Line 9, 1. DEFENDANT ORACLE AMERICA, INC.'S Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award (Notices). Oracle's petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. The court must confirm an arbitration award unless it finds grounds for vacatur. (Code Civ. Proc., section 1286.) The court has denied the petition to vacate in a companion order and therefore confirms the arbitration award. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: 1000 VAN NESS LP VS. 5910 INVESTMENTS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion/Discovery calendar for Monday, May 13, 2026, Line 3, PLAINTIFF 1000 VAN NESS, LP'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff 1000 Van Ness LP's motion to compel compliance with court order and request for sanctions is granted in part and continued for further hearing to June 25, 2026. This court previously granted 1000 Van Ness's motion to compel defendant 5910 Investments LLC's further responses to certain discovery requests. (Davenport Decl., Ex. B.) 5910 Investments has made further responses. (Isola Decl., Exs. A and B.) RFP No. 22: This RFP asks for all contracts for reserved parking in effect at any time between December 1, 2024 and the present. The court ordered 5910 Investments to produce responsive documents in full, but its amended response further narrows the requests to terms common to all contracts, rather than the contracts themselves, plus "report(s)" concerning the contracts. 5910 Investments' response does not comply with the court's prior order. 5910 Investments shall amend to state compliance in full and must produce all of the documents sought by the request. RFP No. 23: The RFP initially requested "All writings showing any money paid under CONTRACTS for reserved parking in the PARKING GARAGE at any time between December 1, 2024 and the present." Because this RFP was overbroad, the court narrowed it to require production only of "balance sheets and financial statements showing payments for parking under reserved parking contracts." 5910 Investments has further narrowed it to "report(s) showing payments received under CONTRACTS for reserved parking in the PARKING GARAGE for the period between December 1, 2024 and February 2026, inclusive." 5910 Investments shall amend to make a representation of compliance for all "balance sheets and financial statements showing payments for parking under reserved parking contracts" for the time period December 1, 2024 and the present. RFP No. 24: The RFP requests "All notices terminating any CONTRACTS for reserved parking in the PARKING GARAGE given at any time between December 1, 2024 and the present." 5910 Investments' statement of compliance says in part that "No notice(s) terminating any CONTRACTS for reserved parking in the PARKING GARAGE were given by Responding Party at any time between December 1, 2024 and the date of this response." This is a sufficient statement of compliance and no further response is ordered. Special Interrogatory Nos. 13-15: 5910 Investments' invocations of Code Civ. Proc., section 2030.230 are insufficient because the production made does not permit the interrogatory to be answered in full by reference to those documents. Defendant shall amend. 5910 Investments shall amend and produce all responsive documents by May 29, 2026. Its production must include native-format production of any ESI that was previously produced in non-native format. 1000 Van Ness seeks monetary sanctions and what it deems "self-executing issue sanctions." The court concludes that 5910 Investments has not complied with the court's prior order and awards monetary sanctions of $8000 payable to 1000 Van Ness within 30 days of this order. 1000 Van Ness seeks a much larger amount that the court deems unreasonable. The court continues the motion for hearing on evidentiary or issue sanctions to June 25, 2026. Following 5910 Investments' amendment and production, the parties are ordered to meet and confer about any outstanding issues. If 1000 Van Ness seeks further relief, it may file a supplemental brief by June 12, 2026. If 5910 Investments opposes further relief, it may file a supplemental brief by June 19, 2026. (Part 1 of 2, tentative ruling continues in Part 2 of 2)

rulings.Case Title: OLGA DASHDORJ VS. MARTIN EBERT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Olga Dashdorjs Request For Admissions To Defendant Martin Ebert, Set One, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion/Discovery calendar for Monday, May 13, 2026, Line 4, 1. PLAINTIFF OLGA DASHDORJ'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Olga Dashdorjs Request For Admissions To Defendant Martin Ebert, Set One, And For Sanctions. Plaintiff Olga Dashdorj's motion to compel further responses to her requests for admission is granted in part and denied in part. Dashdorj's meet and confer efforts were sufficient. The parties narrowed their disputes but some disputes remained. Dashdorj was not required to continue her efforts where the parties' positions were not likely to continue to change. RFA Nos. 1, 2, 8, 9, 11, 12, 16, 41, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47: These RFAs ask defendant Martin Ebert to admit or deny certain propositions about the accident giving rise to this case. Ebert states that he has made a reasonably diligent inquiry but that he cannot admit or deny because he has insufficient personal knowledge in light of his memory loss; he also explains why various documents provided him to by Dashdorj do not enable him to admit or deny. In some instances Ebert claims a lack of knowledge. Upon review of each of the RFA responses, no further response is ordered. A party must respond completely and nonevasively to proper RFAs, but Ebert has answered these in full. A motion to compel further responses does not lie to require a party to change the content of an RFA answer. Rather, the propounding party may seek the cost of proof after trial when a party refuses to admit propositions that are sufficiently clear. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2033.420.) RFA Nos. 5, 7, 10, 14, 15, 18, 31, 33, 36, 38, 50: Ebert's objections lack merit; the RFAs are sufficiently clear that they can and should be answered. Ebert shall amend and provide a substantive response. RFA No. 6, 17, 19, 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, 32, 37, 48, 49: Ebert's objections have merit; no further response is ordered. Ebert shall make the further responses ordered herein within two weeks and shall amend his responses to Form Interrogatory 17.1 where appropriate where further responses have been made to RFAs. Both parties seek sanctions. The court concludes that both sides' positions were partially justified and does not award sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: OLGA DASHDORJ VS. MARTIN EBERT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Olga Dashdorjs Form And Special Interrogatories To Defendant Martin Ebert, Set One, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion/Discovery calendar for Monday, May 13, 2026, Line 4, 2. PLAINTIFF OLGA DASHDORJ Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Olga Dashdorjs Form And Special Interrogatories To Defendant Martin Ebert, Set One, And For Sanctions. Plaintiff Olga Dashdorj's motion to compel further responses to her form and special interrogatories is granted in part. Many of the interrogatories at issue on this motion ask about Martin Ebert's speed. Ebert takes the position that he does not need to respond with data from his Garmin device because that would be expert opinion. This is incorrect. Information is not shielded from ordinary discovery because it is also might be the subject of expert opinion. Moreover, a Garmin device is used by cyclists to track their rides, including their speeds, without resort to experts to understand and use. Special Interrogatory Nos. 1, 2, 3, 29, 30, 31, 32: Ebert's objections lack merit, including his objection that the interrogatory calls for expert opinion or is premature. Special Interrogatory Nos. 7, 8, 9; Form Interrogatory Nos. 12.1: Ebert's response is sufficient and no further response is ordered. A party can be compelled to give a complete and nonevasive answer to a discovery request, but it cannot be compelled to give a substantively different answer. Form Interrogatory No. 12.2: Ebert shall amend. If Ebert has not conducted any witness interviews, he must so state. If he has conducted some, and maintains that work product protects his selection of whom to interview, he must provide more information to enable Dashdorj to evaluate the work product claim. Form Interrogatory No. 15.1: Ebert's objection that the question is premature lacks merit; he shall amend. Form Interrogatory Nos. 16.1, 16.2, 16.3, 16.4, 16.5, 16.6, : No further response is ordered at this time. In light of the early stage of the case, the court sustains Ebert's objections. Nothing in this order prevents Dashdorj from re-serving these form interrogatories later in the case. Form Interrogatory Nos. 20.1, 20.3, 20.4, 20.8, 20.9: Ebert's objections that these interrogatories cannot be used in a bicycle-pedestrian accident lack merit. Ebert shall amend. Dashdorj's meet and confer efforts were adequate; the court agrees that the parties were at impasse at the time the motion was filed. Ebert shall amend where ordered within two weeks. Both parties seek sanctions. Although the court has not ordered full relief, some of the objections Ebert has persisted in defending in his opposition to this motion lack justification entirely and the opposition to this motion lacks substantial justification. The court orders Ebert to pay $3000 to Dashdorj within 30 days. The court has reduced the sanction to what it deems a reasonable amount. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: AMEEN AFSHINPOUR VS. CRAFT CALIFORNIA LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion/Discovery calendar for Monday, May 13, 2026, Line 6, DEFENDANTS CRAFT CALIFORNIA LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, CRAFTY LLC, AN UNKOWN ENTITY'S Motion To Compel Arbitration. Defendant Crafty California LLC ("Crafty")'s motion to compel arbitration is granted. (The Court's complete tentative ruling was emailed to the parties.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
